fireworks.debug.description: Write diagnostic traces to the Fireworks output channel.

fireworks.inlineResults.enabled.markdownDescription: When on, the extension watches the project's `.fireworks/results/` directory and shows each `?` call's value as a read-only inline decoration at the end of its source line. Toggle with the `Fireworks: Toggle Inline Results` command.

fireworks.inlineResults.gap.markdownDescription: Spaces between the last character of code on a line and the inline `?` value.

fireworks.inlineResults.maxLength.markdownDescription: Character budget for inline `?` values on a single line, shared as evenly as possible when a line has more than one result (truncated with `...`). At most 3 results show per line; the rest collapse into a `+ n more` tail. Set to `0` for no limit.

fireworks.inlineResults.color.markdownDescription: Foreground color of the inline `?` value, as any CSS color (e.g. `#e0af68`, `gold`, `rgb(224,175,104)`). Leave empty to use the editor's foreground color, which adapts to light and dark themes. See `#fireworks.inlineResults.opacity#`.

fireworks.inlineResults.opacity.markdownDescription: Opacity of the inline `?` value. Clamped to the range 0.5–1.0 so the value stays legible.

fireworks.inlineResults.backgroundOpacit.markdownDescription: Opacity of a faint background tint behind the inline `?` value (the value color mixed into transparent via CSS `color-mix`). Clamped to 0–0.1; set to `0` for no background. Requires a VS Code build whose Chromium supports `color-mix`.

fireworks.inlineResults.fadeInMs.markdownDescription: Duration in milliseconds of the fade-in when an inline `?` value appears. Set to `0` to show values instantly without animation.

fireworks.liveCoding.terminal.markdownDescription: Where the test-refresh watcher runs. `integrated` uses a dedicated VS Code terminal (Stop/Restart fully supported). `external` launches your own emulator via `#fireworks.liveCoding.externalCommand#` (Start only — Stop/Restart can't be driven by the extension).

fireworks.liveCoding.externalCommand.markdownDescription: Command template for `external` terminal mode, with `${cwd}` and `${command}` placeholders. Example (Ghostty on macOS): `open -na ghostty --args --working-directory="${cwd}" -e ${command}`.

fireworks.liveCoding.fireworksVersion.description: Fireworks version injected into the watcher via clojure -Sdeps.

fireworks.liveCoding.testRefreshVersion.description: test-refresh version injected into the watcher via clojure -Sdeps.

fireworks.liveCoding.injectMacros.markdownDescription: When on, the live-coding watcher interns Fireworks' `?` and `!?` macros into `clojure.core` at startup, so they resolve unqualified in reloaded namespaces without a `:require`. Affects the watcher process only (not your editor/Calva REPL). Off by default — interning into `clojure.core` is global.

scripts.compile: npm run compile-cljs && npm run compile-ts
scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run release-cljs && npm run bundle
